steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: Venter et al. recommend that "reducing anthropogenic emissions remain the primary focus" rather than expanding green space. The question asks which finding best explains why green space alone is insufficient.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'reduced overall effectiveness': names a result without explaining the mechanism. ✗
  • Choice B — 'dense tree cover on busy streets blocks ventilation': identifies the precise failure — tree cover undermines dispersion, the more effective mechanism, exactly where pollution is worst. ✓
  • Choice C — 'vegetation selection as a solution': suggests green space can still fix the problem with the right plants — contradicts the recommendation to prioritize emissions cuts. ✗
  • Choice D — 'external weather as the limiting factor': shifts blame to weather, not a flaw in green space itself. ✗
That matches choice B — B is correct.
